seoMetaDescription: Stay updated with Bronze Tech news including new product releases, industry trends, technical insights, and company announcements.

articles.title: Bronze Tech Expands High-Voltage Connector Line for EV and Solar Applications
articles.subtitle: New CUS-BTB-HV series supports up to 1500V DC for demanding power electronics applications
articles.description: Bronze Tech announces the expansion of its high-voltage connector portfolio with new ratings up to 1500V DC, addressing growing demand from EV charging and solar inverter markets.
articles.author: Marketing Team
articles.content:
Bronze Tech today announced the expansion of its high-voltage board-to-board connector line, with new models supporting up to 1500V DC operation. The enhanced CUS-BTB-HV series addresses the growing demand from electric vehicle charging infrastructure and solar power system manufacturers who require reliable high-voltage interconnects.

The new connectors feature enhanced creepage distances up to 12mm and clearance distances up to 10mm, exceeding industry standards for high-voltage safety. The PBT GF30 housing material provides excellent tracking resistance with CTI ratings above 600V.

'We're seeing rapid growth in the EV and renewable energy sectors, and customers need connectors that can safely handle higher voltage levels,' said Dr. Thomas Anderson, Principal Engineer at Bronze Tech. 'Our expanded high-voltage line gives designers the safety margins they need for next-generation power electronics.'

The new connectors are available in 12, 20, and 32-pin configurations, with current ratings up to 15A per contact. All models carry UL and VDE certifications for high-voltage applications. Samples are available now with production lead times of 8-10 weeks.

articles.title: Top Industrial Automation Trends Driving Connector Demand in 2024
articles.subtitle: Industry 4.0, predictive maintenance, and edge computing reshape interconnect requirements
articles.description: Analysis of key trends in industrial automation and their impact on connector selection and design requirements.
articles.author: James Wilson
articles.content:
The industrial automation landscape continues to evolve rapidly, with several key trends driving new requirements for interconnect solutions. As we move through 2024, these trends are reshaping how engineers approach connector selection.

**Industry 4.0 and IIoT Expansion**

The adoption of Industry 4.0 principles and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) devices continues to accelerate. This drives demand for M12 connectors with higher pin counts to support both power and data transmission. We're seeing increased adoption of M12 X-code connectors for 10 Gigabit Ethernet applications in machine vision and high-speed data acquisition systems.

**Predictive Maintenance Integration**

Manufacturers are increasingly implementing predictive maintenance systems that require additional sensors and connectivity. This trend favors modular connector solutions that can be easily expanded and maintained. M8 connectors are gaining popularity for new sensor installations due to their compact size.

**Edge Computing Growth**

The shift toward edge computing in industrial environments requires reliable high-speed connectivity closer to the production line. This drives demand for shielded M12 D-code and X-code connectors that can maintain signal integrity in electrically noisy environments.

**Sustainability Focus**

Environmental regulations and corporate sustainability goals are pushing manufacturers to specify connectors with extended operating life and recyclable materials. Bronze Tech's commitment to RoHS compliance and extended mating cycle ratings addresses these requirements.

articles.title: Bronze Tech Launches Enhanced M8 Connector Series for Compact Sensors
articles.subtitle: New CIR-M8 series offers improved sealing and higher pin density for space-constrained applications
articles.description: Bronze Tech introduces an enhanced M8 circular connector series with improved IP67 sealing and 8-pin configuration options.
articles.author: Product Marketing
articles.content:
Bronze Tech has launched an enhanced M8 circular connector series designed specifically for compact industrial sensors and actuators. The new CIR-M8 series addresses the growing demand for smaller connectors as sensors continue to miniaturize.

The enhanced series features improved IP67 sealing with a new gasket design that maintains reliability through 200+ mating cycles. Available in 3, 4, and 8-pin configurations, the connectors support current ratings up to 4A per contact.

'As sensors get smaller, customers need connectors that fit in tighter spaces without sacrificing reliability,' said Lisa Chen, FAE for Sensor Applications at Bronze Tech. 'Our new M8 series delivers the same industrial-grade performance as M12 in a more compact package.'

Key features of the new series include:
- Nickel-plated brass housings for EMI protection
- Gold-plated contacts for reliable connections
- Operating temperature range of -25°C to +85°C
- A-coded standard configuration
- Panel mount and cable mount options

The CIR-M8 series is available now with standard lead times.

articles.title: Bronze Tech Achieves IATF 16949 Certification for Automotive Applications
articles.subtitle: Quality management certification enables expanded supply to automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers
articles.description: Bronze Tech receives IATF 16949 certification, opening opportunities for supplying connectors to automotive industry customers.
articles.author: Quality Assurance Team
articles.content:
Bronze Tech is pleased to announce that it has achieved IATF 16949:2016 certification, the international quality management standard specific to the automotive industry. This certification recognizes Bronze Tech's commitment to quality and continuous improvement in the design and manufacturing of interconnect products.

The IATF 16949 certification is required by most automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers, and its achievement opens significant new market opportunities for Bronze Tech. The certification covers the company's entire product line, including board-to-board, wire-to-board, and circular connectors.

'Automotive applications demand the highest levels of quality and reliability,' said the Quality Assurance Director at Bronze Tech. 'This certification validates our quality management systems and demonstrates our capability to meet the stringent requirements of the automotive industry.'

The certification process involved a comprehensive audit of Bronze Tech's quality management systems, manufacturing processes, and product development procedures. The company implemented several improvements during the certification journey, including enhanced traceability systems and expanded testing capabilities.

With IATF 16949 certification, Bronze Tech can now pursue opportunities with automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers for applications including EV charging systems, battery management, and onboard electronics.
